title: Sock It to Down Syndrome Day
story: People around the world wear mismatched socks on March 21 to celebrate World Down Syndrome Day! This colorful tradition helps spread awareness and acceptance for people with Down Syndrome. Schools, offices, and families join in by wearing crazy sock combinations - polka dots with stripes, or rainbow socks with animal prints. The date March 21 was chosen because Down Syndrome involves three copies of the 21st chromosome. Social media fills with photos of funky feet as people share messages of inclusion and friendship. It's a simple way to show that differences make our world more interesting and beautiful.
activity_question: Why do people wear mismatched socks on World Down Syndrome Day?
activity_choices: To start a new fashion trend
To make their feet more comfortable
follow_up_question: What other creative ways could people show support for inclusion and acceptance?

title: Trees Get Their Own Special Day!
story: Every March 21, people around the world celebrate International Day of Forests by planting trees, taking nature walks, and sharing photos of their favorite forests online. Schools organize tree-planting ceremonies while families visit local parks to appreciate the amazing trees around them. Some communities host forest clean-up events or build fairy houses at the base of old oak trees. It's a day to remember that forests give us clean air, homes for animals, and the paper we write on every day!
activity_question: What do people commonly do to celebrate International Day of Forests?
activity_choices: Plant trees and take nature walks
activity_answer: Plant trees and take nature walks
follow_up_question: What's your favorite thing about forests that you'd want to protect?

title: Rhyme Time Around the World
story: Poets everywhere grab their pens and share their favorite verses on March 21 for World Poetry Day! Schools host poetry slams where kids recite funny poems about pizza or friendship. Libraries organize open mic nights for anyone brave enough to share their rhymes. Social media fills with haikus, limericks, and heartfelt poems in dozens of languages. Some people write brand new poems just for this special day, while others rediscover old favorites tucked away in books.
activity_question: What type of poetry events do schools often host on World Poetry Day?
follow_up_question: What would you write a poem about if you celebrated World Poetry Day?

title: Spring's Sweet New Beginning
story: Every March 21, millions of people celebrate Nowruz, the Persian New Year that marks the first day of spring! Families gather around beautiful tables called "haft-seen" decorated with seven special items that start with the letter S, like apples, coins, and sprouted wheat. People jump over small fires to leave behind bad luck and welcome good fortune. This ancient celebration brings together communities who share delicious rice dishes, give gifts, and spend two weeks visiting friends and relatives to celebrate fresh starts.
activity_question: What do people jump over during Nowruz celebrations?
follow_up_question: What would you put on your own special celebration table?
